trigger_condition: Multiple users with same email (case-insensitive duplicates)
guard_snippet: users = User.objects.filter(email__iexact=email); user = users.first() if users.count()==1 else None
potential_consequence: Wrong account targeted; reset link emailed for an unintended user.

trigger_condition: Inactive/disabled user (is_active=False) still receives reset link
potential_consequence: Disabled accounts can be re-enabled by setting a new password.

trigger_condition: Email field validates but contains uppercase or unicode confusables
potential_consequence: Throttle key (lower) and DB lookup (iexact) diverge from confusable matches; bypasses rate limit.

trigger_condition: Invalid-payload path skips throttle increment effectively
guard_snippet: increment throttle counter even when serializer invalid
potential_consequence: Attacker spams malformed bodies to probe timing of generic-200 path without rate limit penalty.

trigger_condition: Invalid email format returns 200 with no timing parity to send-email path
guard_snippet: add constant-time delay or async send to equalize response time
potential_consequence: Timing side-channel leaks whether email exists/sent vs validation-failed.

trigger_condition: make_token raises if user.password is unusable (set_unusable_password)
potential_consequence: Unhandled exception leaks 500 differing from generic-200 contract; enumeration.

trigger_condition: SAFENET_FRONTEND_URL unset/empty in env
potential_consequence: Reset URL becomes '/reset-password/...' relative; users land on broken link.

trigger_condition: user.account access raises (RelatedObjectDoesNotExist not caught by hasattr in all Django versions)
potential_consequence: Unhandled exception during DLL fallback leaks differing 500 response.

trigger_condition: DeadLetterLog.objects.create fails when account is None and field is non-nullable
guard_snippet: only create DLL if account FK can accept null or skip
potential_consequence: Inner try swallows but loses error context; DLL row never written.

trigger_condition: User has no related account (registered before account creation)
guard_snippet: explicit None handling already present, but audit metadata still references user.id without account
potential_consequence: Audit entry exists with account=None — operator console may not surface event.

trigger_condition: write_audit_event DB failure after email already sent
guard_snippet: wrap audit write in try/except to avoid 500 after successful send
potential_consequence: User receives email but request returns 500 — repeated retries spam Resend.

trigger_condition: uid or token is non-string (list/dict from JSON)
guard_snippet: if not isinstance(request.data.get('uid'), str): return invalid
potential_consequence: AttributeError on .strip() bubbles to 500 instead of INVALID_RESET_LINK.

trigger_condition: urlsafe_base64_decode produces bytes that force_str cannot decode (binary garbage)
potential_consequence: UnicodeDecodeError uncaught — 500 response.

trigger_condition: Decoded user_pk is non-numeric string but User.objects.get accepts int conversion failure
potential_consequence: DB driver may raise ProgrammingError not in except tuple → 500.

trigger_condition: User exists but is_active=False
potential_consequence: Disabled user can complete password reset and regain access.

trigger_condition: check_token raises (e.g., user.password is None)
potential_consequence: Unhandled exception → 500 differing from 400 contract.

trigger_condition: new_password missing entirely from payload
potential_consequence: Validation error message may surface 'This field is required' but VALIDATION_ERROR with field=null doesn't help frontend.

trigger_condition: Concurrent confirm requests with same valid token
potential_consequence: Race: two requests both pass check_token, both set passwords; second overwrites first silently.

trigger_condition: set_password+save without revoking outstanding JWT refresh tokens
potential_consequence: Stolen refresh tokens remain valid after password reset (FR43 intent violated).

trigger_condition: Active sessions for user are not invalidated
guard_snippet: Session.objects.filter(...) delete or rotate session key
potential_consequence: Attacker session persists after victim resets password.

trigger_condition: DB save fails after set_password mutated in-memory user
potential_consequence: In-memory state diverges from DB; subsequent code paths may use stale hash.

trigger_condition: Audit write fails after password already changed
potential_consequence: Password changed but no audit trail; 500 returned to user who already has new password.

trigger_condition: No notification email sent confirming password change
potential_consequence: Victim of unauthorized reset never learns their password changed.

trigger_condition: Token check timing differs by user-existence vs token-mismatch
guard_snippet: always run check_token even on get-failure with dummy user
potential_consequence: Timing side-channel reveals which uids correspond to existing users.

trigger_condition: request.data fails to parse (DRF lazy parse error in throttle phase)
guard_snippet: try: request.data except ParseError: return ip-based key
potential_consequence: Throttle raises before view; client receives 500/parse error instead of 400.

trigger_condition: Email field is a list/dict (JSON injection)
potential_consequence: AttributeError on .strip() → throttle bypasses with anonymous IP key.

trigger_condition: Same email submitted with varying whitespace/case bypasses already-strip-lower normalization
potential_consequence: Unicode confusables produce different cache keys; rate limit bypass.

trigger_condition: get_ident returns None when behind proxy without X-Forwarded-For
potential_consequence: Cache key 'throttle_password_reset_anon_None' lumps all anonymous attackers under one bucket.

trigger_condition: Confirm-throttle keys only by IP; attacker behind shared NAT locks out legitimate users
guard_snippet: key by IP+uid hash to scope per-target
potential_consequence: Mass false-positive rate-limits for users behind same egress.

trigger_condition: Confirm-throttle does not key on uid → cannot detect distributed token brute-force
potential_consequence: Per-IP 5/min still allows attackers using residential proxy pool to brute force token space.

trigger_condition: new_password has no max_length — extremely long password DoS
potential_consequence: Argon2 hashing of multi-MB string consumes CPU/memory.

trigger_condition: Password matches but is identical to current password
potential_consequence: User can reset to existing password — UX-confusing.

trigger_condition: context['user'] missing (programmer error)
guard_snippet: if user is None: raise ValidationError('user context required')
potential_consequence: validate_password called with None — UserAttributeSimilarityValidator silently no-ops, weakening checks.

trigger_condition: Resend.Emails.send returns dict missing 'id' but with error key
guard_snippet: if isinstance(result,dict) and 'error' in result: raise
potential_consequence: RuntimeError generic — actual API error not surfaced to logs.

trigger_condition: user.email contains uppercase — RFC 5321 local-part is case-sensitive
guard_snippet: recipient = user.email.strip()  # do not lowercase local-part
potential_consequence: Mail to lowercased address may bounce.

trigger_condition: user.email is None on legacy rows
guard_snippet: already handled by 'user.email or' but user attribute itself could be missing
potential_consequence: AttributeError if user model differs.

trigger_condition: resend.Emails.send raises non-Exception (network timeout)
potential_consequence: Caller view catches Exception broadly — but specific timeouts mean DLL row contains opaque message.

trigger_condition: reset_url no length cap
potential_consequence: Mail clients may truncate links silently; user sees broken link.

trigger_condition: User submits empty/whitespace email — required attr satisfied by browser only
potential_consequence: POST sent with empty body; backend returns generic 200 but wastes throttle budget for IP key.

trigger_condition: Double-submit (user clicks twice rapidly) before loading state effective
guard_snippet: if (loading) return; at top of handleSubmit
potential_consequence: Two requests fire — second consumes 1/3 hourly throttle budget; legitimate retries blocked.

trigger_condition: Network offline — error swallowed, user shown 'sent' generic
potential_consequence: User believes email sent when network never delivered request.

trigger_condition: Backend returns 429 throttled — caught silently and user sees success message
guard_snippet: check err.response.status===429 and surface 'try again later'
potential_consequence: User waits forever for an email that the throttle prevented being sent.

trigger_condition: params.uid or params.token undefined (route mounted without segments)
potential_consequence: Backend returns INVALID_RESET_LINK only after submit — wasted user effort entering passwords.

trigger_condition: params.uid/token contain URL-encoded chars passed verbatim
potential_consequence: Server-side decode may differ; valid token rejected.

trigger_condition: VALIDATION_ERROR for 'non_field_errors' falls into general error
potential_consequence: Confusing UX.

trigger_condition: Back-button reveals filled password fields in DOM
guard_snippet: clear state before router.replace; use unmount
potential_consequence: Password remains in browser back history.

trigger_condition: Server returns 429 throttled — falls into generalError generic message
potential_consequence: User keeps clicking — throttle window never clears; perceived broken UI.

trigger_condition: Path '/forgot-password-evil' startsWith '/forgot-password' bypasses auth
potential_consequence: Attacker registers route '/forgot-password-admin' that bypasses authentication.

trigger_condition: Matcher excludes '/reset-password' but route uses dynamic segments
potential_consequence: If matcher regex misfires page redirects to /register.

trigger_condition: password_reset throttle scope cleared on cache flush/restart
guard_snippet: use persistent throttle backend, not LocMemCache
potential_consequence: Backend restart resets throttle; attacker times restarts to bypass.

trigger_condition: PASSWORD_RESET_TIMEOUT and frontend reset-link copy ('1 hour') drift
guard_snippet: derive frontend copy from settings or test asserts equality
potential_consequence: Spec mismatch — link copy says 1 hour but backend reads different value.

trigger_condition: force_bytes(user.pk) where pk is UUID/non-int
potential_consequence: For UUID pks the b64 encode includes hyphens — confirm endpoint's int() parsing fails.

trigger_condition: Trailing slash on SAFENET_FRONTEND_URL stripped, but path lacks leading slash if base contains query string
potential_consequence: Malformed URL when SAFENET_FRONTEND_URL has query/fragment.

trigger_condition: Cache-Control set after Response constructed but DRF middleware may overwrite
guard_snippet: also set Pragma: no-cache and Expires: 0 for legacy proxies
potential_consequence: HTTP/1.0 proxies cache success page revealing token consumption state.
